postCopy.instagram: Deep in the Appalachian hills, someone talked. Now someone's dead. 🥃🔍 Case #043 — Blood and Bourbon. Play free at thedailywhodunit.com
postCopy.tiktok: Free daily murder mystery game: a Prohibition-era moonshine operation, a body in the still house, and a traitor nobody saw coming. New murder mystery puzzle every day — solve it at thedailywhodunit.com

marketing.imagePrompt: Interior of an Appalachian mountain moonshine distillery in 1922, copper stills gleaming in lantern light, rough-hewn timber walls, mason jars lined on shelves. A man's boots visible beneath an overturned barrel. Dense forest visible through a cracked wooden door, mist on the ridge beyond. Color palette: amber, deep shadow brown, lantern gold, forest green. Gritty and atmospheric, style of Andrew Wyeth meets Gordon Parks.
marketing.animationPrompt: Slow push through dense Appalachian forest at dusk toward a weathered still house, lantern light bleeding through the cracks. The door swings open a fraction — no one exits. Hold on a copper still, steam rising, a mason jar fallen and shattered on the dirt floor. The lantern inside flickers once and goes dark. Fade to black.

rookie.title: Blood and Bourbon
rookie.setting: Harlow Still House · Caldwell County · Appalachian Mountains · 1922
rookie.flavor: Elden Harlow ran the most profitable moonshine operation in Caldwell County — until someone put him face-down in the dirt beside his own still. The revenuers didn't do it. The door was locked from the inside and only three people had a key. One of them is a killer.
rookie.emptyState: The still house was locked. The killer had a key.

items.name: Cora Harlow
items.detail: Elden's wife, runs the books and holds her own key.

items.name: Dutch Vane
items.detail: Elden's chief runner and the only man who knew every delivery route.

items.name: Silas Prout
items.detail: The still operator who maintains the copper and mixes the mash.

items.name: The Still House
items.detail: Where Elden's body was found, door locked from within.

items.name: The Root Cellar
items.detail: Underground storage for finished product beneath the farmhouse.

items.name: The Ridge Path
items.detail: The only trail connecting the still house to the main road.

items.name: Copper Mash Paddle
items.detail: A heavy stirring tool found wiped clean beside the still.

items.name: Mason Jar
items.detail: Shattered jar — broken edge consistent with a laceration wound.

items.name: Bootlegger's Pistol
items.detail: A .38 revolver found beneath the overturned barrel near the body.

clues.heading: Three Keys, One Lock
clues.text: The still house door was locked from the inside using a drop bar. Entry requires either a key to lift the exterior latch simultaneously or knowledge of a hidden secondary release. Cora, Dutch, and Silas each have a key. The hidden release was known only to Elden and whoever he trusted enough to show.
clues.alone: Only someone with a key or knowledge of the hidden release could have entered and locked the still house from within.

links.insight: The still house requiring a key or hidden release to lock from inside and Dutch Vane's key found worn smooth on the latch side confirm Dutch regularly used his key to enter the still house — including on the night of the murder.

clues.heading: The Wiped Paddle
clues.text: The copper mash paddle found beside the still shows fresh wiping along the blade — residue consistent with blood removed hastily. Trace blood in the copper rivets at the paddle's joint could not be fully cleaned. The blood type matches Elden Harlow's.
clues.alone: The mash paddle was used to strike Elden and hastily wiped — the killer tried to clean the weapon but missed the rivets.

links.insight: The mash paddle wiped of Elden's blood and Silas Prout's confirmed tavern alibi together confirm Silas did not use the paddle — the killer was someone else with still house access that night.

clues.heading: Cora's Evening
clues.text: A neighbouring farm wife confirms Cora Harlow spent the evening of the murder at her kitchen table, visible through an uncurtained window, from supper until well past the time Elden's body was found. The neighbour's husband corroborates the account independently.
clues.alone: Cora Harlow was at the farmhouse kitchen all evening — two independent witnesses confirm she never left.

links.insight: Cora's confirmed presence at the farmhouse kitchen and Dutch Vane's absence from his own home during the same window together identify Dutch as the only key holder unaccounted for on the night of Elden's death.

clues.heading: The Revenue Tip
clues.text: A federal revenue agent's field report, obtained through a county contact, records an anonymous tip received four days before Elden's murder — detailing the still house's location, operating schedule, and Elden's weekly movements. The tip was specific enough to come from someone inside the operation.
clues.alone: Someone inside Harlow's operation tipped off the revenuers four days before the murder — a betrayal from within.

links.insight: The revenue tip containing operational details only an insider could know and the payment record showing Dutch Vane deposited sixty dollars four days before the murder confirm Dutch sold the information and then killed Elden before the raid could expose him as the source.

clues.heading: The Worn Key
clues.text: Dutch Vane's key to the still house shows unusual wear on the latch-contact edge — consistent with frequent use of the exterior latch mechanism. Silas Prout's key shows wear primarily on the bow, suggesting he rarely used the latch side. Cora's key shows balanced wear.
clues.alone: Dutch Vane used his still house key at the latch far more frequently than Silas — he was a regular entrant, not just an occasional one.

links.insight: The still house requiring a key or hidden release to lock from inside and Dutch Vane's key found worn smooth on the latch side confirm Dutch regularly used his key to enter the still house — including on the night of the murder.

clues.heading: Silas at the Tavern
clues.text: Silas Prout was confirmed at Hatcher's tavern in town from mid-afternoon until closing — a six-hour stretch witnessed by the barman and four other patrons. He arrived before Elden was last seen alive and did not leave the tavern until after the body was discovered.
clues.alone: Silas Prout was at the tavern all evening with multiple witnesses — he could not have been at the still house.

links.insight: The mash paddle wiped of Elden's blood and Silas Prout's confirmed tavern alibi together confirm Silas did not use the paddle — the killer was someone else with still house access that night.

clues.heading: The Ridge Path Sighting
clues.text: A trapper working the upper ridge confirms seeing a man matching Dutch Vane's description — broad-shouldered, wearing a canvas coat — walking the ridge path toward the still house at dusk. He did not see the man return. Dutch claims he spent the evening at home but his wife was visiting her sister in the next county.
clues.alone: Dutch Vane was seen on the ridge path toward the still house at dusk — and his alibi witness was absent.

links.insight: Cora's confirmed presence at the farmhouse kitchen and Dutch Vane's absence from his own home during the same window together identify Dutch as the only key holder unaccounted for on the night of Elden's death.

clues.heading: The Unexplained Deposit
clues.text: A Caldwell County bank record shows Dutch Vane deposited sixty dollars in cash four days before the murder — an amount equivalent to three weeks' runner wages with no documented source. Dutch cannot account for the money when questioned. The deposit date matches the day the revenue tip was filed.
clues.alone: Dutch Vane deposited unexplained cash on the exact day the revenue tip was filed — consistent with payment for the information.

links.insight: The revenue tip containing operational details only an insider could know and the payment record showing Dutch Vane deposited sixty dollars four days before the murder confirm Dutch sold the information and then killed Elden before the raid could expose him as the source.

rookie.epilogue: Dutch Vane had sold the still house location to the revenuers for sixty dollars — enough to start over somewhere the law wasn't closing in. When Elden confronted him about the deposit, Dutch grabbed the mash paddle and struck him down, then locked the door behind him using the exterior latch. The trapper's sighting, the worn key, and the bank deposit date unravelled him. He was arrested at the Virginia state line two days later.

detective.title: Blood and Bourbon
detective.setting: Harlow Still House · Caldwell County · Appalachian Mountains · 1922
detective.flavor: Elden Harlow is dead, Dutch Vane's alibi is tissue-thin, and the revenuers are two days away from raiding an operation someone already burned. But Dutch didn't act alone in selling out the still — and the second set of footprints near the root cellar suggests someone else knew exactly what was coming.
detective.emptyState: Dutch pulled the trigger. Find out how he got in — and who else knew.

items.name: Dutch Vane
items.detail: Chief runner, ridge path sighting, unexplained deposit.

items.name: Cora Harlow
items.detail: Elden's wife — confirmed at the farmhouse, but controls the books.

items.name: Silas Prout
items.detail: Still operator — tavern alibi, but knows every inch of the operation.

items.name: Harmon Cade
items.detail: The operation's silent financier, based in Lenoir, never at the still.

items.name: The Still House
items.detail: Primary crime scene — locked from inside, body found at the still.

items.name: The Root Cellar
items.detail: Underground storage — second set of footprints found here.

items.name: The Ridge Path
items.detail: Only trail to the still house — Dutch seen here at dusk.

items.name: The Farmhouse Kitchen
items.detail: Where Cora was confirmed all evening by two witnesses.

items.name: Copper Mash Paddle
items.detail: Found wiped — Elden's blood in the copper rivets.

items.name: Mason Jar
items.detail: Shattered — edge consistent with a secondary laceration.

items.name: Bootlegger's Pistol
items.detail: Found under the barrel — unfired, Dutch's initials on the grip.

items.name: Strangling Wire
items.detail: A length of baling wire found coiled near the still — bruising on neck.

items.name: Latch Key
items.detail: One of three keys that operates the exterior latch mechanism.

items.name: Hidden Release
items.detail: A concealed lever Elden showed only to his most trusted associates.

items.name: Roof Hatch
items.detail: A maintenance access panel in the still house roof — rarely used.

items.name: Invited Entry
items.detail: Elden opened the door himself — he trusted whoever came.

clues.heading: The Latch Evidence
clues.text: The exterior latch mechanism shows fresh scoring on the inner guide rail — consistent with the latch key being used to lift the bar from outside while the drop bar was simultaneously positioned. This sequence requires two hands coordinating from outside the door, which is only possible with the latch key. Dutch's key matches the scoring pattern.
clues.alone: The door was locked using the latch key from outside after the drop bar was set — Dutch's key matches the fresh scoring on the guide rail.

links.insight: The latch scoring matching Dutch's key and the trapper's sighting placing Dutch on the ridge path at dusk confirm he approached the still house, entered, committed the murder, and locked the door behind him using his key from outside.

clues.heading: The Paddle and the Blow
clues.text: The forensic examination of Elden's wound confirms a single blunt strike to the rear of the skull — delivered from behind while Elden faced the still. The mash paddle's blade dimension matches the wound precisely. Dutch's initials — D.V. — are scratched into the paddle's handle, establishing it as his working tool.
clues.alone: The mash paddle is Dutch's tool and matches the fatal wound — he struck Elden from behind while Elden's back was turned.

links.insight: Dutch's initials on the murder weapon and the bank deposit on the day the revenue tip was filed confirm Dutch both sold Elden out and killed him when confronted — the betrayal preceded the murder by four days.

clues.heading: Two Alibis Confirmed
clues.text: Cora Harlow's kitchen alibi is corroborated by a second independent witness — a farmhand who passed the window at 9 pm and saw her at the table. Silas Prout's tavern alibi is confirmed by the barman's written log and four patron statements. Both are definitively cleared as direct killers.
clues.alone: Cora and Silas are both confirmed clear of the still house that evening — Dutch is the only unaccounted key holder.

links.insight: Cora and Silas cleared by confirmed witnesses and Harmon Cade never present at the still house confirm Dutch as the sole person with physical access and the unaccounted evening window to commit the killing.

clues.heading: The Revenue Report
clues.text: The federal revenue agent's field report details the still house coordinates, Elden's Tuesday and Friday operating schedule, and the root cellar's hidden entrance — information that required direct physical knowledge of the site. Harmon Cade has never visited the still. Silas and Cora are cleared. Only Dutch and Elden had all three pieces of knowledge.
clues.alone: The revenue tip contained site-specific details that only Dutch or Elden could have provided — and Elden didn't tip his own operation.

links.insight: The revenue tip containing details only Dutch possessed and the root cellar footprints matching a second person's boots confirm someone else accessed the cellar after learning the raid was coming — likely to remove their own financial stake before the revenuers arrived.

clues.heading: The Trapper's Account
clues.text: The trapper on the upper ridge provides a detailed account: Dutch Vane on the ridge path at dusk, moving quickly, canvas coat, no lantern. The trapper noted the time because he was checking his own traps by last light. He estimated Dutch reached the still house clearing within fifteen minutes of the sighting. Dutch was never seen returning.
clues.alone: Dutch was on the ridge path at dusk heading to the still house and was never seen leaving — his movements place him at the scene during the window of death.

links.insight: The latch scoring matching Dutch's key and the trapper placing Dutch on the ridge path at dusk confirm he approached the still house, entered, committed the murder, and locked the door behind him using his key from outside.

clues.heading: Sixty Dollars and a Date
clues.text: The Caldwell County bank deposit record shows sixty dollars cash deposited by Dutch Vane on a Tuesday — the same day the revenue tip was logged at the federal office in Lenoir. The amount is consistent with a paid informant's standard rate for operational intelligence in the region, per a county sheriff's notation.
clues.alone: Dutch deposited the informant rate on the exact day the tip was filed — the timing is not coincidental.

links.insight: Dutch's initials on the murder weapon and the bank deposit on the day the revenue tip was filed confirm Dutch both sold Elden out and killed him when confronted — the betrayal preceded the murder by four days.

clues.heading: The Root Cellar Prints
clues.text: A second set of boot prints was found in the root cellar — a narrow size, distinct from Dutch's broad work boot and from Elden's worn field boot. The prints lead to the cellar's inner shelf, where six cases of finished product were stored. Two cases are missing. The prints do not match Cora, Silas, or Dutch.
clues.alone: A fourth person accessed the root cellar and removed two cases of product — someone outside the confirmed suspect pool knew about the cellar and the raid.

links.insight: Cora and Silas cleared by witnesses and an unidentified fourth person's prints in the root cellar confirm the operation had an unknown party with specific site knowledge — pointing to Harmon Cade acting through an intermediary.

clues.heading: Cade's Withdrawal
clues.text: A letter recovered from Elden's coat — undelivered — was addressed to Harmon Cade and informed him that Dutch had been seen meeting with a federal contact in Lenoir. The letter was dated three days before the murder, suggesting Elden suspected Dutch and was warning Cade — who then had his own reason to act before exposure.
clues.alone: Elden had identified Dutch as the informant and was alerting Cade — giving Cade advance warning to protect his investment before the raid.

links.insight: The revenue tip containing details only Dutch possessed and the root cellar prints confirming a fourth party accessed the product confirm Cade received advance warning and sent someone to retrieve his financial stake before the revenuers arrived.

detective.epilogue: Dutch Vane sold the still house to the revenuers, pocketed sixty dollars, and planned to be long gone before the raid. When Elden confronted him with the letter he'd drafted to Cade, Dutch grabbed the mash paddle and struck him from behind, then used the latch key to lock the still house from outside. Harmon Cade — warned by Elden's undelivered letter which Dutch found in the coat — sent a man to clear the root cellar before the raid arrived. Dutch was arrested at the Virginia line. Cade was indicted six months later.

inspector.title: Blood and Bourbon
inspector.setting: Harlow Still House · Caldwell County · Appalachian Mountains · 1922
inspector.flavor: Dutch killed Elden and Cade salvaged his investment. But the revenue tip that started everything came with a guarantee — someone promised Dutch that the raid wouldn't happen until he was clear. That promise came from inside the federal office. There is a fourth party in this conspiracy who has never been near Caldwell County.
inspector.emptyState: Dutch killed. Cade protected his money. Someone else made the whole thing possible.

items.name: Dutch Vane
items.detail: The killer — sold out the operation and murdered Elden.

items.name: Cora Harlow
items.detail: Cleared as killer — but the books show a second income stream.

items.name: Silas Prout
items.detail: Cleared at the tavern — but his cousin works the federal office.

items.name: Harmon Cade
items.detail: Silent financier — warned by Elden's letter, sent man to root cellar.

items.name: Agent Roy Finch
items.detail: Federal revenue agent in Lenoir — the one who received the tip.

items.name: The Still House
items.detail: Primary crime scene — Dutch's key, Dutch's paddle, Dutch's murder.

items.name: The Root Cellar
items.detail: Two cases removed — narrow boot print, Cade's man confirmed.

items.name: The Ridge Path
items.detail: Dutch's approach route — confirmed by the upper ridge trapper.

items.name: The Farmhouse Kitchen
items.detail: Cora's confirmed location — but where the ledger books are kept.

items.name: The Federal Office in Lenoir
items.detail: Where the tip was filed — and where the raid delay was arranged.

items.name: Copper Mash Paddle
items.detail: Dutch's tool — confirmed murder weapon, Elden's blood in rivets.

items.name: Mason Jar
items.detail: Shattered — secondary laceration on Elden's left forearm.

items.name: Bootlegger's Pistol
items.detail: Dutch's pistol — found unfired, suggests paddle was opportunistic.

items.name: Strangling Wire
items.detail: Coiled near the still — neck bruising suggests it was attempted first.

items.name: Lye Solution
items.detail: Still cleaning chemical — trace burn on Elden's right hand.

items.name: Latch Key
items.detail: Dutch's key — latch scoring confirms exterior lock after the murder.

items.name: Hidden Release
items.detail: Elden's concealed lever — shown only to most trusted associates.

items.name: Roof Hatch
items.detail: Maintenance panel — found with fresh mud on the frame exterior.

items.name: Invited Entry
items.detail: Elden opened the door — he expected Dutch for a confrontation.

items.name: Cellar Tunnel
items.detail: A crawl passage from the root cellar to the still house foundation.

items.name: Filed the Revenue Tip
items.detail: Submitted the informant intelligence to the federal office.

items.name: Delayed the Raid
items.detail: Ensured the federal raid was postponed to give Dutch time to flee.

items.name: Cleared the Root Cellar
items.detail: Sent a man to remove Cade's financial stake before the raid.

items.name: Warned the Killer
items.detail: Informed Dutch that Elden knew — triggering the confrontation.

items.name: Destroyed the Ledger
items.detail: Removed financial records linking the operation to outside parties.

clues.heading: The Delayed Raid Order
clues.text: The federal raid on the Harlow still house was originally scheduled for the day after the tip was filed. An internal memorandum — obtained through a county attorney — shows the raid was postponed for five days at the request of Agent Roy Finch, citing operational confirmation needs. No confirmation was ever sought or filed. The delay gave Dutch exactly the window he needed.
clues.alone: Agent Finch delayed the raid by five days without documented cause — the postponement was arranged to benefit whoever tipped him, not the federal investigation.

links.insight: Finch's undocumented raid delay and the payment ledger showing Finch received forty dollars from an account linked to Cade's Lenoir business three days after the tip confirm Finch was paid to manage the timeline and protect Dutch's escape window.

clues.heading: The Roof Hatch Mud
clues.text: Fresh red clay mud was found on the exterior frame of the still house roof hatch — the maintenance access panel. The mud matches the composition of the ridge path above the still house, not the still house floor. The hatch had not been opened in months based on its rusted hinge — except recently, when someone cleaned the hinge and re-rusted it with water.
clues.alone: The roof hatch was recently opened and disguised to look undisturbed — someone used it to observe the still house interior without being seen.

links.insight: The roof hatch used for observation and Silas Prout's cousin employed at the Lenoir federal office together confirm the operation was under surveillance from inside before the tip was filed — the intelligence was gathered covertly, not volunteered.

clues.heading: Cora's Second Ledger
clues.text: A second ledger found beneath a loose board in the farmhouse kitchen — discovered during an extended search — records a series of payments made to R.F. over eight months, totalling one hundred and twenty dollars. The payments correspond to dates when the federal revenue patrol was redirected away from Caldwell County roads. Cora was paying for protection.
clues.alone: Cora Harlow had been paying Agent Roy Finch for eight months to redirect federal patrols — she was the operation's silent protector, not just the bookkeeper.

links.insight: Cora's eight-month payment ledger to Finch and the torn tip document showing Finch's handwriting on the operational details confirm Finch was Cora's contact — and when Dutch approached him directly with a tip, Finch played both sides simultaneously.

clues.heading: Silas's Cousin
clues.text: Extended questioning of Silas Prout reveals his first cousin, Ernie Prout, works as a records clerk at the Lenoir federal revenue office. Silas admits he mentioned the still house's location to Ernie in conversation three months prior — not as a tip, but as family talk. Ernie's desk is the one that logged the incoming anonymous tip.
clues.alone: Silas's cousin Ernie works the desk that received the tip and knew the still house location from a family conversation — the intelligence chain runs through the Prout family to the federal office.

links.insight: Ernie Prout's desk receiving the tip and his signed statement confirming Agent Finch instructed him to log it as anonymous despite it arriving as a named submission confirm Finch controlled the tip process and protected Dutch's identity as the source.

clues.heading: Finch's Payment Record
clues.text: A Lenoir bank account linked to Agent Roy Finch received a forty-dollar deposit three days after Dutch's tip was filed — traceable to a business account registered to Harmon Cade's Lenoir lumber company. The deposit note reads consultation fee. Cade had paid Finch to manage the raid timing and protect the cellar stock removal window.
clues.alone: Harmon Cade paid Agent Finch forty dollars three days after the tip — Finch was simultaneously taking Cora's protection money and Cade's raid-delay payment.

links.insight: Finch's undocumented raid delay and the payment from Cade's account three days after the tip confirm Finch was paid to manage the timeline — he delayed the raid to give Dutch a window to flee and Cade's man a window to clear the cellar.

clues.heading: The Cellar Tunnel
clues.text: A crawl passage connecting the root cellar to the still house foundation — known only to Elden and Cora — was found with a fresh scrape in the dirt floor consistent with a body being dragged. The tunnel was not used to move Elden's body, but the scrape suggests someone used it to move product out of the still house and into the cellar after the murder — before accessing the cellar from outside.
clues.alone: The tunnel was used after the murder to move product from the still house into the cellar — the cellar removal operation was coordinated with knowledge of the tunnel, which only Cora and Elden knew about.

links.insight: The roof hatch used for covert observation and the cellar tunnel used post-murder by someone with inside knowledge confirm the operation had been surveilled and mapped in advance — the intelligence Finch received was more detailed than a casual tip.

clues.heading: The Tip Document
clues.text: The original tip document recovered from the Lenoir federal archive shows it was submitted not anonymously but under the name D. Vance — a slight alteration of Dutch Vane. Agent Finch's own handwriting appears in the margin notation log as anonymous — source protected. Finch actively concealed Dutch's identity to protect his informant.
clues.alone: Dutch submitted the tip under a thin alias and Finch deliberately logged it as anonymous — Finch was protecting Dutch as a controlled informant, not just a one-time tipster.

links.insight: Cora's eight-month payment ledger to Finch and the tip document showing Finch concealed Dutch's identity confirm Finch was running a protection racket on both sides — taking Cora's money to divert patrols and Dutch's tip to build a case, playing all parties against each other.

clues.heading: Ernie Prout's Statement
clues.text: Ernie Prout, confronted with the tip document bearing his desk stamp, provides a signed statement: Agent Finch handed him the tip document already completed and instructed him to stamp it as received and log it as anonymous. Finch told Ernie the source was a protected informant. Ernie did not know Dutch Vane was the source until shown the D. Vance alias.
clues.alone: Agent Finch personally controlled every step of the tip's processing — he handed it to Ernie pre-completed and ensured it was logged to protect Dutch as his asset.

links.insight: Ernie Prout's signed statement confirming Finch controlled the tip document and Silas's cousin working the receiving desk confirm the entire intelligence chain from family conversation to federal office was managed by Finch to extract money from every party simultaneously.

inspector.epilogue: Dutch Vane sold the still house, killed Elden, and ran. Agent Roy Finch took money from Cora for eight months of patrol diversions, took Cade's money to delay the raid, and protected Dutch as a controlled informant — running a three-way extraction from the same operation. Cade sent a man through the cellar tunnel to clear his product before the revenuers arrived. Silas's cousin Ernie was an unwitting conduit. Dutch was arrested at the Virginia line. Finch was indicted for corruption eighteen months later after Ernie's testimony. Cade settled a civil forfeiture and was never charged criminally. Cora kept the farm.
